message:
feat(locks): implement file reservation lifecycle commands

Adds complete file reservation lifecycle management for multi-agent coordination:

- Add ForceReleaseReservation client method to agentmail package
- Add ForceReleaseOptions and ForceReleaseResult types
- Restructure ntm locks command with subcommands:
  - locks list: show active reservations (with reservation IDs)
  - locks force-release: forcibly release stale reservations
  - locks renew: extend TTL of active reservations
- Add confirmation prompts and previous holder notification for force-release

Also includes cleanup:
- Remove unused functions in supervisor, tmux, and dashboard packages
- Fix redundant .Copy() calls on immutable lipgloss styles

Closes: ntm-sor1, ntm-21dx, ntm-7o58, ntm-iyqu

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-1ts","title":"Implement ntm lock/unlock file reservation commands","description":"# Implement ntm lock/unlock File Reservation Commands

## Purpose

Expose Agent Mail's file reservation system as NTM commands, allowing agents to declare intent before editing files and avoid conflicts.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Reserve files for editing
ntm lock myproject "src/api/**" --reason "Implementing user endpoints"

# Reserve multiple patterns
ntm lock myproject "src/api/**" "tests/api/**"

# With TTL (default 1 hour)
ntm lock myproject "config/*.json" --ttl 2h

# Shared (read) vs exclusive (write) lock
ntm lock myproject "docs/**" --shared

# Release specific locks
ntm unlock myproject "src/api/**"

# Release all locks for session
ntm unlock myproject --all

# Show current locks
ntm locks myproject
ntm locks myproject --all-agents  # Include other sessions
```

## Lock Display Format

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ File Reservations: myproject                                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 🔒 src/api/**                                               │
│   Agent: ntm_myproject │ Exclusive │ Expires in 45m        │
│   Reason: Implementing user endpoints                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 🔒 tests/api/**                                             │
│   Agent: ntm_myproject │ Exclusive │ Expires in 45m        │
│   Reason: Implementing user endpoints                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 📖 docs/** (shared)                                         │
│   Agent: GreenCastle │ Shared │ Expires in 1h30m           │
│   Reason: Updating API documentation                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Conflict Detection

When attempting to lock:
```
⚠️ Conflict detected:
  Pattern: src/api/**
  Held by: GreenCastle (cc_1)
  Reason: Refactoring auth module
  Expires in: 25 minutes

Options:
  1. Wait for expiry
  2. Request release: ntm mail send myproject --to cc_1 "Need src/api/**"
  3. Force lock (dangerous): ntm lock myproject "src/api/**" --force
```

## Integration with spawn/add

When spawning agents:
```bash
# Check for conflicts before spawning
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --check-locks

# Auto-reserve working directory
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --reserve-cwd
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lock patterns with glob support
- [ ] TTL specification (default 1h, max 24h)
- [ ] Exclusive vs shared locks
- [ ] Conflict detection with helpful messages
- [ ] ntm locks command shows current state
- [ ] --all-agents shows all project reservations
- [ ] Integration hooks for spawn command","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCastle","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:42.508839-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:33.733237-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:33.733237-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-1ts","depends_on_id":"ntm-yxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.109325-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-20n","title":"Implement --robot-assign for work distribution recommendations","description":"AI-assisted work assignment suggestions based on current state.

## Rationale
With N agents and M ready beads, optimal assignment is non-trivial. This flag analyzes current state and recommends which agent should work on what, considering:
- Agent types and their strengths
- Current workload balance  
- Bead dependencies and priorities
- Recent activity patterns

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-assign myproject
ntm --robot-assign myproject --beads=ntm-abc,ntm-def  # specific beads
ntm --robot-assign myproject --strategy=balanced      # or: speed, quality
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "strategy": "balanced",
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "agent": "0.1",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "assign_bead": "ntm-1rz",
      "confidence": 0.85,
      "reasoning": "Profiler task matches claude's code analysis strengths"
    },
    {
      "agent": "0.2", 
      "agent_type": "codex",
      "assign_bead": "ntm-7mg",
      "confidence": 0.72,
      "reasoning": "UI/styling task, codex handles CSS well"
    }
  ],
  "blocked_beads": [
    {"id": "ntm-xyz", "blocked_by": ["ntm-abc"]}
  ],
  "idle_agents": ["0.3"],
  "unassignable_beads": [
    {"id": "ntm-qqq", "reason": "Requires human input"}
  ]
}
```

## Assignment Strategies
- balanced: distribute work evenly across agents
- speed: assign to fastest/most active agents  
- quality: match task types to agent strengths
- dependency: prioritize unblocking work

## Integration with Beads
Requires parsing bd ready and bd show output to understand task graph. Consider caching bead state to avoid repeated queries.

## Future Enhancement
Could integrate with agent-mail to check agent's stated preferences or current focus.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Gemini","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:51:50.71632-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T12:21:24.943748-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T18:39:56.329873881-06:00","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-assign with strategies (balanced, speed, quality, dependency), agent-task matching, and structured JSON output. Added 6 test functions covering all helper functions."}

 {"id":"ntm-22f","title":"Implement pre/post command hooks","description":"Run user scripts before and after NTM commands.

## What This Task Does

Enable users to define hooks that run before or after specific
NTM commands. Hooks can log, notify, validate, or transform.

## Configuration

# ~/.config/ntm/config.toml

[hooks]
# Run before any send command
pre_send = "~/.config/ntm/hooks/log-prompt.sh"

# Run after spawn completes
post_spawn = "notify-send 'NTM' 'Session created'"

# Run before dangerous commands
pre_kill = "~/.config/ntm/hooks/confirm-kill.sh"

## Hook Interface

Hooks receive command context as JSON on stdin:
{
  "command": "send",
  "args": ["myproject", "--all", "hello"],
  "session": "myproject",
  "flags": {"all": true},
  "timestamp": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z"
}

Post hooks also receive result:
{
  "command": "spawn",
  "result": "success",
  "session": "myproject",
  "panes_created": 4,
  "duration_ms": 1234
}

## Hook Execution

Pre hooks:
- Run synchronously before command
- Non-zero exit blocks the command
- Stdout captured but not displayed (log only)
- Stderr shown to user

Post hooks:
- Run asynchronously after command
- Exit code doesn't affect NTM
- Good for notifications, logging

## Disabling Hooks

For scripting or sensitive operations:
$ ntm send --no-hooks myproject 'message'

## Example Hooks

Log all prompts:
#\!/bin/bash
jq -c '{time: .timestamp, session: .session, prompt: .args[2]}' >> ~/.ntm-prompts.jsonl

Confirm before kill:
#\!/bin/bash
session=$(jq -r '.session' -)
read -p "Really kill $session? [y/N] " confirm
[[ "$confirm" == "y" ]] || exit 1

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Hooks defined in config
- [ ] Pre hooks run before command
- [ ] Pre hook failure blocks command
- [ ] Post hooks run after command
- [ ] Command context passed as JSON
- [ ] --no-hooks flag disables hooks

 {"id":"ntm-239","title":"Implement ntm health command for agent status checking","description":"Create a command to check health status of all agents in a session.

## What This Task Does

Create 'ntm health' command that checks and reports agent health:
- Process status (running/exited)
- Activity level (active/idle/stale)
- Error detection (rate limits, crashes)
- Output summary with visual indicators

## Command Interface

$ ntm health myproject
Session: myproject
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Pane │ Agent   │ Status  │ Activity │ Issues  │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 0    │ cc_1    │ ✓ OK    │ active   │ -       │
│ 1    │ cc_2    │ ✓ OK    │ idle     │ -       │
│ 2    │ cod_1   │ ⚠ WARN  │ stale    │ no output 5m │
│ 3    │ gmi_1   │ ✗ ERROR │ crashed  │ exit 1  │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Overall: 2 healthy, 1 warning, 1 error

$ ntm health myproject --json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "checked_at": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z",
  "agents": [...],
  "summary": {"healthy": 2, "warning": 1, "error": 1}
}

## Health Detection Logic

Process status:
- Check if pane has running process (tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}')
- Check process state via /proc or ps

Activity detection:
- Capture pane content, compare over time
- 'active': Content changed recently (< 30s)
- 'idle': No change, but process running (prompt visible)
- 'stale': No change for extended period (> 5m)

Error detection (regex patterns):
- Rate limit: 'rate limit', '429', 'too many requests'
- Auth error: 'authentication', 'unauthorized', '401'
- Crash: 'error', 'exception', 'panic', exit codes

## Implementation

1. Create internal/health package
2. Implement process status checker
3. Implement activity detector
4. Implement error pattern matcher
5. Create ntm health command with TUI output
6. Add --json support (depends on ntm-ws2)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm health shows all agents with status
- [ ] Process crashes are detected
- [ ] Rate limit patterns are detected
- [ ] Activity level shown (active/idle/stale)
- [ ] Exit code reflects overall health
- [ ] --json output supported

 {"id":"ntm-7lk","title":"SUB-EPIC: Integration Tests","description":"# SUB-EPIC: Integration Tests

## Scope
Integration tests that verify component interactions across package boundaries.

## Key Integration Points

### 1. CLI → tmux Integration
- spawn command creates real sessions with correct pane layout
- send command delivers prompts to correct panes
- interrupt command sends Ctrl+C to all agent panes
- status command reports accurate pane states

### 2. CLI → config Integration
- Commands load config from correct locations
- Config changes affect command behavior
- Default values work correctly

### 3. robot → tmux Integration
- --robot-status outputs accurate session info
- --robot-plan suggests valid actions
- JSON output is parseable and accurate

### 4. status → tmux Integration
- Agent state detection works with real pane output
- Error detection triggers correctly
- Prompt detection works across agent types

### 5. palette → config Integration
- Palette loads commands from config
- Custom palette files are honored
- Categories are properly organized

### 6. output → cli Integration
- --json flag enables JSON output across commands
- Text output formatting is consistent
- Error output goes to correct stream

## Test Approach
- Use real tmux sessions (cleanup on test end)
- Use temp directories for config
- Capture and verify actual output
- Test error conditions explicitly

## Prerequisites
- Unit tests for individual packages should pass first
- tmux must be installed on test system","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:50.889846-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T12:21:24.946511-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T22:37:11.132743-05:00","close_reason":"Integration tests implemented and passing: cli_tmux_test.go, config_test.go, status_test.go - tests session spawn/send/status, config loading, status detection","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-7lk","depends_on_id":"ntm-l5h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:05.947564-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-7lk","depends_on_id":"ntm-idx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:06.114595-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-7nk3","title":"EPIC: Checkpoint & Rollback System","description":"# Checkpoint & Rollback System

## Background & Motivation

AI agents sometimes go off track. By the time users notice, significant work may be wasted.

Checkpoints provide quick recovery:
1. Create named save points before risky operations
2. Roll back to known-good state when things go wrong

## What Gets Checkpointed

### Captured (for restoration)
- **Git state**: Uncommitted changes saved as patch
- **Session layout**: Pane configuration, agent types

### Captured (for reference only)
- **Pane scrollback**: What agents were doing (NOT restored)
- **Timestamp and description**: Human context

### NOT Captured
- Agent internal state (we can't access it)
- API conversation history (we can't access it)

## IMPORTANT: What Rollback Does

Rollback restores GIT STATE, not agent state.

```bash
ntm rollback myproject "before-refactor"
```

This will:
1. ✅ Stash current uncommitted changes
2. ✅ Apply checkpoint's git patch
3. ✅ Interrupt all agents (Ctrl+C)
4. ⚠️ Agents restart with FRESH context (no memory of pre-rollback work)

**Critical understanding**: Agents don't "remember" the checkpoint. They start fresh. The checkpoint restores FILES, not agent memory.

## Command Interface

```bash
# Create checkpoint
ntm checkpoint myproject "before-refactor"
# Captures: git diff, session layout, scrollback (for reference)

# List checkpoints
ntm checkpoints myproject
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Checkpoints: myproject                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ before-refactor    │ 15 min ago │ 3 files │ 4 agents      │
│ api-working        │ 2 hrs ago  │ 1 file  │ 4 agents      │
│ auto-20251210-1400 │ 3 hrs ago  │ 8 files │ 4 agents      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

# Preview rollback
ntm rollback myproject "before-refactor" --dry-run

# Execute rollback
ntm rollback myproject "before-refactor"
# Warning: This will reset files and interrupt agents.
# Current changes will be stashed. Continue? [y/N]

# Quick rollback to last checkpoint
ntm rollback myproject --last
```

## Storage Format

```
~/.config/ntm/checkpoints/myproject/before-refactor/
├── metadata.json         # Timestamp, description, session info
├── git-state.patch       # git diff output
├── session-layout.json   # Pane configuration
└── scrollback/           # Reference only (not restored)
    ├── cc_1.txt
    └── cod_1.txt
```

## Auto-Checkpoints

Configurable automatic checkpoints:
```toml
[checkpoints]
auto_enabled = true
auto_events = ["broadcast_send"]  # Before ntm send --all
max_auto_checkpoints = 10
retention_days = 7
```

## Validation

Before rollback:
- Check if checkpoint exists
- Warn if checkpoint is old (>24h)
- Warn if git state has diverged significantly
- Validate patch can apply

## Success Criteria

- [ ] ntm checkpoint creates named checkpoints
- [ ] Git state (uncommitted) captured as patch
- [ ] Session layout captured for reference
- [ ] Scrollback captured for reference (not restored)
- [ ] ntm rollback restores git state
- [ ] Agents interrupted on rollback (fresh start)
- [ ] Auto-checkpoint on configurable events
- [ ] Retention policy with rotation
- [ ] Clear user messaging about what rollback does/doesn't do","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:28:29.179411-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T17:26:47.971388-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T17:26:47.971388-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-7pl","title":"Unit Tests: internal/status package (69.6% → 85%)","description":"Unit Tests: internal/status package (70.6% → 83.7%)

## Final Status
- Initial coverage: 70.6%
- Final coverage: 83.7%
- Target was 85%

## What was added
- Tests for AgentState.String() and ErrorType.String()
- Tests for AddPromptPattern and AddErrorPattern
- Tests for RecoveryManager: GetRecoveryEvents, ResetAll, pruneEvents, SendRecoveryPrompt
- Tests for BuildContextAwarePrompt and GetBeadContext
- Tests for IsPromptLine edge cases (literal matching, agent type filtering)
- Tests for DetectIdleFromOutput edge cases
- Tests for Detect and DetectAll with real tmux sessions (skip if unavailable)
- Integration tests for CompactionRecoveryIntegration

## Remaining gap (1.3%)
Functions requiring real tmux sessions or bv tool:
- unified.go: Detect (14.3%), DetectAll (55.6%) - require tmux calls
- recovery.go: GetBeadContext (68.8%) - requires bv tool installed

These are integration-level functions that cannot be fully covered in unit tests without mocks (which this project doesn't use).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacMountain","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:21.080727-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:53:59.507447-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:53:59.507447-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-7pl","depends_on_id":"ntm-idx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:22:34.242755-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-ix0s","title":"Task: Test TOML config file loading from disk","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:45:32.006284-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:45:32.006284-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ix0s","depends_on_id":"ntm-u4s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:45:45.266474-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-iye0","title":"Implement output capture with tagging","description":"Capture agent outputs with metadata for comparison and analysis. Enables output synthesis and structured extraction.

OUTPUT CAPTURE:

1. RAW CAPTURE:
   - Capture full pane output at intervals
   - Store with timestamp, pane ID, associated prompt
   - Ring buffer: keep last N captures per pane (configurable)

2. STRUCTURED EXTRACTION:
   
   a) CODE BLOCKS:
      - Extract markdown code blocks: \`\`\`lang ... \`\`\`
      - Tag with language and line numbers
      - Store separately for easy access
   
   b) JSON OUTPUTS:
      - Detect JSON objects/arrays in output
      - Parse and validate
      - Store structured for field access
   
   c) FILE PATHS:
      - Extract file paths mentioned in output
      - Pattern: src/..., ./..., internal/...
      - Tag with action (created, modified, deleted)
   
   d) COMMANDS:
      - Extract shell commands (lines starting with $ or %)
      - Tag with exit code if visible

DATA STRUCTURES:
```go
type CapturedOutput struct {
    PaneID       string
    Timestamp    time.Time
    RawContent   string
    Prompt       string      // associated prompt if known
    
    // Extracted structures
    CodeBlocks   []CodeBlock
    JSONOutputs  []json.RawMessage
    FilePaths    []FileMention
    Commands     []CommandMention
}

type CodeBlock struct {
    Language    string
    Content     string
    LineStart   int
    LineEnd     int
}

type FileMention struct {
    Path   string
    Action string  // created, modified, deleted, read
}
```

STORAGE:
- In-memory: circular buffer per pane
- Optional file persistence: .ntm/captures/<session>/<pane>/<timestamp>.json
- Configurable retention: keep last N or last T duration

INTEGRATION WITH ACTIVITY:
- Trigger capture on state transitions (GENERATING → WAITING)
- This ensures we capture complete outputs, not mid-stream

LOCATION: internal/robot/synthesis.go

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Raw output captured with metadata
- Code blocks extracted correctly
- JSON detected and parsed
- File paths extracted with actions
- Storage respects retention limits
- Capture triggered on state transitions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:02:14.518410834-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T20:44:47.614240188-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-iye0","depends_on_id":"ntm-sp0c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:02:28.678296898-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-iymd","title":"Feature: Test Strategy Implementation","description":"# Test Strategy Implementation

Orchestrators fail at the seams. The test strategy must specifically cover integration boundaries and failure modes.

## Test Categories

### 1) Tool Contract Tests (when tools are installed)
Run in CI when full ecosystem available:
- Version parsing tests
- Capability detection tests
- Schema validation with golden files

### 2) Deterministic Fake Tools (always in CI)
Fake binaries in testdata/faketools/ that simulate:
- Normal operation
- Timeout (sleep forever)
- Partial output (truncated JSON)
- Schema change (different field names)
- Non-zero exit codes
- Missing binary

### 3) Daemon Chaos Tests
Test supervisor resilience:
- Restart on crash
- Port collision handling
- Health check flapping
- Max restart backoff

### 4) End-to-End Session Tests
Full integration with real tmux:
- Session lifecycle (spawn → work → terminate)
- Event emission verification
- Crash recovery (close store, reopen, verify state)

### 5) Policy Enforcement Tests
- Blocked command logging
- Approval workflow

## Files to Create
- internal/tools/contract_test.go
- testdata/faketools/ (bv, bd, am, cm, cass, s2p fakes)
- internal/supervisor/chaos_test.go
- internal/session/e2e_test.go
- internal/policy/enforcement_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Contract tests for all 6 tools
- [ ] Fake tool binaries for CI
- [ ] Daemon chaos tests passing
- [ ] E2E session test passing
- [ ] Policy enforcement tests passing","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:22:40.759308-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T23:05:03.064505-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T23:05:03.064505-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented test strategy foundation: fake tools, contract tests, chaos tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-iymd","depends_on_id":"ntm-wotk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:22:48.227438-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-j0ax","title":"Task: Create PATH wrappers for git","description":"Create .ntm/bin/git wrapper that checks policy before executing real git.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# .ntm/bin/git
POLICY_FILE="\${POLICY_FILE:-\$PWD/.ntm/policy.yaml}"
NTM_LOG="\${NTM_LOG:-\$PWD/.ntm/logs/blocked.jsonl}"

check_blocked() {
    local cmd="\$*"
    # Check against policy (uses Go binary for real impl)
    if echo "\$cmd" | grep -qE "(reset --hard|checkout --|clean -f)"; then
        echo "🛑 BLOCKED by NTM policy" >&2
        echo "{\"ts\":\"\$(date -Iseconds)\",\"cmd\":\"\$cmd\",\"blocked\":true}" >> "\$NTM_LOG"
        exit 1
    fi
}

check_blocked "\$@"
exec /usr/bin/git "\$@"
```

The wrapper is installed by ntm init and prepended to PATH in spawn.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:48:17.539317-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T23:16:38.269484-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T23:16:38.269484-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in internal/cli/safety.go - gitWrapperScript and rmWrapperScript are created by 'ntm safety install'","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-j0ax","depends_on_id":"ntm-io2c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:48:17.95534-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-sk07","title":"Feature: CASS Historical Context Injection","description":"# CASS Historical Context Injection

## The Opportunity
CASS indexes 50K+ sessions across 11 different agent types (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Roo, Cline, Windsurf, etc.) with sub-60ms search.

NTM could inject relevant historical context before spawning agents, so they don't reinvent solutions.

## CASS Capabilities
- Multi-agent indexing (11 agent types)
- Full-text search
- Semantic search (embedding-based)
- Hybrid search (combined)
- Multi-machine unified index

## Implementation (internal/context/historical.go)

### Search Function
```go
type CASSMatch struct {
    SessionID   string
    AgentType   string
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Score       float64
    Snippet     string
    FilesEdited []string
}

func searchHistoricalContext(task string, limit int) ([]CASSMatch, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("cass", "search",
        "--query", task,
        "--limit", fmt.Sprintf("%d", limit),
        "--mode", "hybrid",
        "--json",
    )
    // Graceful degradation if CASS unavailable
}
```

### Context Pack Integration
CASS results included in the ContextPack built before task assignment:
- Budget: 15% of total context budget
- Truncated if exceeds budget
- Cached by (repo_rev, task, limit)

## CASS Commands
```bash
cass search "authentication error" --robot --limit 5
cass view /path/to/session.jsonl -n 42 --json
cass expand /path/to/session.jsonl -n 42 -C 3 --json
```

## New NTM Commands
```bash
ntm context history "implement JWT auth"
ntm context history --agent=claude --days=7
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CASS search wrapper implemented
- [ ] Results included in ContextPack
- [ ] Token budget enforced (15%)
- [ ] Graceful degradation if CASS unavailable
- [ ] Filters: agent type, recency","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:18:57.5946-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T20:18:57.5946-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-sk07","depends_on_id":"ntm-dwuv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:56.12452-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sk07","depends_on_id":"ntm-6hxh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:19:57.022706-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-sor1","title":"Feature: File Reservation Lifecycle","description":"# File Reservation Lifecycle

## The Problem
NTM spawns multiple agents on the same codebase with NO file coordination. Result: merge conflicts, lost work, frustrated developers.

## The Solution
Advisory file locks via Agent Mail:
1. RESERVE before assigning work
2. RENEW heartbeat while working
3. RELEASE after completion
4. FORCE-RELEASE if agent crashes (with approval)

## Lifecycle
```
Reserve → Work → Release
    ↓
  Conflict? → Find alternative work OR wait
    ↓
  Stale? → Force-release (requires approval)
```

## Implementation

### Reserve Before Assignment
```go
func assignWorkWithReservations(ctx, session, agent, bead) {
    files := predictAffectedFiles(bead)
    reservations := agentmail.ReservePaths(ctx, files)
    if len(reservations.Conflicts) > 0 {
        // Find alternative work or wait
    }
    // Assign work
}
```

### Predict Affected Files
Use bv --robot-impact if available, otherwise use label-to-pattern mapping.

### Release After Completion
```go
func OnTaskComplete(ctx, session, agentName) {
    agentmail.ReleaseReservations(ctx, projectPath, agentName)
}
```

### Force-Release Stale (with Approval)
```go
func checkForStaleReservations(ctx) {
    for _, res := range reservations {
        if time.Since(agent.LastActiveTS) > staleTimeout {
            approval := policyChecker.CheckApproval(ctx, "force_release", res.ID)
            if approval.Granted {
                agentmail.ForceReleaseReservation(ctx, res.ID)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Pre-Commit Guards
```bash
ntm guards install    # Install Agent Mail pre-commit hook
ntm guards status     # Check hook status
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm reserve "internal/auth/**/*.go" --agent=GreenLake --ttl=1h
ntm release --agent=GreenLake
ntm reservations list
ntm reservations force-release <id> --reason="Agent crashed"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reserve-before-assign implemented
- [ ] Release-after-complete implemented
- [ ] Force-release with approval workflow
- [ ] Pre-commit guard installation
- [ ] Conflicts detected and handled gracefully","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:40.737974-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:40.737974-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-xz26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:52.39541-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp4g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:52.935065-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-6g41","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:53.112053-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-014q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:27:28.488802-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sor1","depends_on_id":"ntm-gy4o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:28:11.837956-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-srrl","title":"Implement activity summary generation","description":"Generate per-agent activity summaries. Provides overview of what each agent accomplished.

PURPOSE:
After parallel agent work, users need a quick summary of:
- What did each agent work on?
- What files were modified?
- How much output was generated?
- Were there any errors?

SUMMARY DATA:
```go
type ActivitySummary struct {
    Session      string
    TimeRange    TimeRange
    Agents       []AgentSummary
    TotalFiles   int
    Conflicts    []FileConflict
}

type AgentSummary struct {
    PaneID       string
    AgentType    string
    
    // Activity metrics
    ActiveTime   time.Duration  // time in GENERATING state
    IdleTime     time.Duration  // time in WAITING state
    OutputLines  int
    OutputChars  int
    
    // Content summary
    FilesModified []string
    CodeBlocks    int           // count of code blocks produced
    Commands      int           // count of commands run
    
    // Health
    Errors       int
    Restarts     int
    
    // Highlights (extracted from output)
    KeyActions   []string       // e.g., "Created src/auth.go", "Fixed test"
}
```

KEY ACTION EXTRACTION:
Heuristics to identify key actions from output:
- Lines containing: "Created", "Modified", "Fixed", "Added", "Removed"
- Lines with file paths + action verbs
- Summary sections ("Changes made:", "Summary:")
- Limit to top 5 key actions per agent

OUTPUT FORMATS:

1. HUMAN READABLE:
```
Session: myproject (last 30 minutes)

Agent myproject__cc_1 (Claude):
  Active: 12m 30s | Output: 2,450 lines
  Files: src/auth.go, src/user.go, tests/auth_test.go
  Key actions:
    - Created authentication middleware
    - Added JWT token validation
    - Fixed password hashing

Agent myproject__cc_2 (Claude):
  Active: 8m 15s | Output: 1,200 lines
  Files: src/api/routes.go
  Key actions:
    - Added user registration endpoint
```

2. JSON (for robot mode)
Full structured data as above.

CLI COMMAND:
```bash
ntm summary SESSION [--since=30m] [--format=text|json]
```

ROBOT MODE:
```bash
ntm --robot-summary=SESSION --since=30m
```

LOCATION: internal/robot/synthesis.go

DEPENDS ON:
- Output capture with tagging (ntm-iye0)
- File conflict detection (ntm-1m1m)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Summary generated for each active agent
- Metrics calculated correctly
- Key actions extracted from output
- Both text and JSON formats work
- Time range filtering works","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:09.155958446-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:09.155958446-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-srrl","depends_on_id":"ntm-iye0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:18.516174628-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-srrl","depends_on_id":"ntm-1m1m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:18.58044592-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
